title: Underboob Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Assuming gauge: 2 HDC stitches = 1 cm
# Front panel width = 46 cm (92 stitches)
# Nipple distance = 26 cm (52 stitches apart -> Peaks at X=20 and X=72)

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:92, Row = 1:25)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    Part = case_when(
      # The base band (Rows 1 to 4)
      Row <= 4 ~ "Base Band",
      
      # Left Cup: Centers at stitch 20. Width shrinks by 1 on each side per row.
      Row > 4 & Stitch >= (1 + (Row-4)) & Stitch <= (39 - (Row-4)) ~ "Left Cup",
      
      # Right Cup: Centers at stitch 72. Width shrinks by 1 on each side per row.
      Row > 4 & Stitch >= (53 + (Row-4)) & Stitch <= (91 - (Row-4)) ~ "Right Cup",
      
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the stitch map
ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Base Band" = "#2C3E50", "Left Cup" = "#E74C3C", "Right Cup" = "#E74C3C")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + # Keeps our stitches square for the visualization
  labs(
    title = "Crochet Stitch Map: Underboob Top",
    subtitle = "1 Tile = 1 Half Double Crochet (HDC)",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 92 sts = ~46cm)", 
    y = "Row Count (Height)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

text:

title: Underboob Top
text:
Written Pattern
Gauge: 2 HDC = 1 cm
Stitches Used: HDC (Half Double Crochet), Sl St (Slip Stitch), Ch (Chain)

Base Band

Row 1: Foundation HDC 92 (or Ch 93, HDC in 2nd ch from hook and each ch across). (92 sts)
Rows 2 - 4: Ch 1, turn. HDC in each st across. (92 sts)



Left Cup
Worked over the first 39 stitches of the Base Band.

Row 5: With the right side facing you, skip the first stitch. Attach yarn to the 2nd stitch of the Base Band. Ch 1, HDC in the same st and the next 36 sts. Leave the remaining stitches unworked. (37 sts)
Row 6: Turn, Sl St into the first st. Ch 1, HDC in the next 35 sts. Leave the last st unworked. (35 sts)
Rows 7 - 23: Repeat the process from Row 6 (turn, Sl St in the first st, Ch 1, HDC across leaving the last st unworked). Each row will decrease by 2 stitches (1 at the beginning, 1 at the end).
Final Row (Row 23): 1 HDC remains. Fasten off.



Right Cup
Worked over stitches 53 - 91 of the Base Band (leaving 13 stitches unworked between the cups).

Row 5: Skip 13 sts after the base of the Left Cup (sts 40-52). Attach yarn to stitch 54. Ch 1, HDC in the same st and the next 36 sts. Leave the last st (st 92) unworked. (37 sts)
Row 6: Turn, Sl St into the first st. Ch 1, HDC in the next 35 sts. Leave the last st unworked. (35 sts)
Rows 7 - 23: Repeat the process from Row 6, decreasing by 2 sts per row (skipping the first and last stitch) until 1 stitch remains. Fasten off.

title: Crochet Patterns
text:
Welcome to the pattern directory.

Underboob Top
Sneak A Peek Top

title: Technical Crochet Patterns
text:
Welcome to the Technical Crochet Pattern visualization repository.
Here you will find plain-text crochet measurements and stitch descriptions translated into structured R data matrices, visualized using ggplot2.

title: Sneak A Peek Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Gauge: 2 HDC stitches = 1 cm
# Front panel width = 46 cm (92 stitches)

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:92, Row = 1:33)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    Part = case_when(
      Row <= 4 ~ "Base Band",
      
      # Left Cup Lower (Rows 5 to 16)
      # Outer edge shrinks by 1. Inner edge grows by 2.
      Row > 4 & Row <= 16 & 
      Stitch >= (1 + (Row-4)) & 
      Stitch <= (22 + 2*(Row-4)) ~ "Left Cup",
      
      # Left Cup Upper (Rows 17 to 32)
      # Outer edge shrinks by 1. Inner edge shrinks by 1.
      Row > 16 & 
      Stitch >= (13 + (Row-16)) & 
      Stitch <= (46 - (Row-16)) ~ "Left Cup",
      
      # Right Cup Lower (Rows 5 to 16)
      # Inner edge grows by 2. Outer edge shrinks by 1.
      Row > 4 & Row <= 16 & 
      Stitch >= (71 - 2*(Row-4)) & 
      Stitch <= (92 - (Row-4)) ~ "Right Cup",
      
      # Right Cup Upper (Rows 17 to 32)
      # Inner edge shrinks by 1. Outer edge shrinks by 1.
      Row > 16 & 
      Stitch >= (47 + (Row-16)) & 
      Stitch <= (80 - (Row-16)) ~ "Right Cup",
      
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the stitch map
ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Base Band" = "#2C3E50", "Left Cup" = "#9B59B6", "Right Cup" = "#9B59B6")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + 
  labs(
    title = "Crochet Stitch Map: Sneak A Peek Top",
    subtitle = "1 Tile = 1 Half Double Crochet (HDC) | Diamond Cutout Feature",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 92 sts = ~46cm)", 
    y = "Row Count (Height)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

title: Sneak A Peek Top
text:
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Gauge: 2 HDC stitches = 1 cm
# Front panel width = 46 cm (92 stitches)

grid <- expand.grid(Stitch = 1:92, Row = 1:33)

grid <- grid %>%
  mutate(
    Part = case_when(
      Row <= 4 ~ "Base Band",
      
      # Left Cup Lower (Rows 5 to 16)
      # Outer edge shrinks by 1. Inner edge grows by 2.
      Row > 4 & Row <= 16 & 
      Stitch >= (1 + (Row-4)) & 
      Stitch <= (22 + 2*(Row-4)) ~ "Left Cup",
      
      # Left Cup Upper (Rows 17 to 32)
      # Outer edge shrinks by 1. Inner edge shrinks by 1.
      Row > 16 & 
      Stitch >= (13 + (Row-16)) & 
      Stitch <= (46 - (Row-16)) ~ "Left Cup",
      
      # Right Cup Lower (Rows 5 to 16)
      # Inner edge grows by 2. Outer edge shrinks by 1.
      Row > 4 & Row <= 16 & 
      Stitch >= (71 - 2*(Row-4)) & 
      Stitch <= (92 - (Row-4)) ~ "Right Cup",
      
      # Right Cup Upper (Rows 17 to 32)
      # Inner edge shrinks by 1. Outer edge shrinks by 1.
      Row > 16 & 
      Stitch >= (47 + (Row-16)) & 
      Stitch <= (80 - (Row-16)) ~ "Right Cup",
      
      TRUE ~ "Empty Space"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Part != "Empty Space")

# Plotting the stitch map
ggplot(grid, aes(x = Stitch, y = Row, fill = Part)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Base Band" = "#2C3E50", "Left Cup" = "#9B59B6", "Right Cup" = "#9B59B6")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + 
  labs(
    title = "Crochet Stitch Map: Sneak A Peek Top",
    subtitle = "1 Tile = 1 Half Double Crochet (HDC) | Diamond Cutout Feature",
    x = "Stitch Count (Width: 92 sts = ~46cm)", 
    y = "Row Count (Height)"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

title: Sneak A Peek Top
text:
Written Pattern
Gauge: 2 HDC = 1 cm
Stitches Used: HDC (Half Double Crochet), Sl St (Slip Stitch), Ch (Chain)

Base Band

Row 1: Foundation HDC 92 (or Ch 93, HDC in 2nd ch from hook and each ch across). (92 sts)
Rows 2 - 4: Ch 1, turn. HDC in each st across. (92 sts)



Left Cup
Worked over the first 22 stitches of the Base Band. The “inner edge” is the side facing the cleavage. The “outer edge” is the armpit side.
Lower Cup (Increasing Width): - Row 5: With the right side facing you, skip the first stitch (outer edge decrease). Attach yarn to the 2nd stitch. HDC in the next 20 sts. Work 3 HDC in the next st (st 22, the inner edge). Leave the remaining 70 stitches unworked. (23 sts) - Rows 6 - 16: Turn, Ch 1. On the inner edge, work 3 HDC into the first/last stitch (this increases by 2 sts). On the outer edge, skip the first/last stitch by either slip stitching at the beginning of the row or leaving it unworked at the end (this decreases by 1 st). You will gain a net of 1 st per row. By Row 16, you will have 34 sts.
Upper Cup (Decreasing Width): - Rows 17 - 32: Turn, Sl St into the first st. Ch 1, HDC across, leaving the last st unworked. (Each row decreases by 2 sts, one on each edge). - Final Row (Row 33): 2 HDC remain. Fasten off, leaving a long tail for the strap.


Right Cup
Worked over stitches 71 - 92 of the Base Band. This leaves a gap of 48 unworked stitches in the middle to form the base of the diamond cutout.
Lower Cup (Increasing Width): - Row 5: Skip 48 sts from the base of the Left Cup (sts 23-70). Attach yarn to stitch 71 (the inner edge). Work 3 HDC in this first st. HDC in the next 20 sts, leaving the last st (st 92) unworked. (23 sts) - Rows 6 - 16: Turn, Ch 1. Just like the Left Cup, decrease 1 st on the outer armpit edge (by skipping the first/last stitch), and increase 2 sts on the inner cleavage edge (by working 3 HDC into the first/last stitch). You will gain 1 st per row, ending with 34 sts on Row 16. (Note: At Row 16, the inner edges of both cups will naturally meet in the center above the cutout!)
Upper Cup (Decreasing Width): - Rows 17 - 32: Turn, Sl St into the first st. Ch 1, HDC across, leaving the last st unworked. (Decreases by 2 sts per row). - Final Row (Row 33): 2 HDC remain. Fasten off, leaving a long tail for the strap. You may also slip stitch the two cups together where they touch at Row 16 for extra stability.
